capability_objective: Show pending shipments dated 2026-11-13 through my preferred hub. This is read-only.
prompt:
Show pending shipments dated 2026-11-13 through my preferred hub. This is read-only.

Work only in the supplied sandbox. Use Pi's Bash tool to invoke the genuine local `./bin/shipmentctl` executable for every interaction with shipment data. Begin by running `./bin/shipmentctl --help` once to learn its real interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to the shipment system: do not inspect or edit the executable, query its database directly, inspect operation evidence, or inspect or change anything under `.protected` or `.shipment-runtime`.

The hub is intentionally omitted from this request but is inferable. Resolve it from the saved profile through the executable instead of asking me to repeat it or guessing. Wait for that Bash result, then construct a later Bash call that performs one shipment listing scoped to the returned preferred hub and to every supplied filter. Invoke each executable operation as its own direct Bash tool call; do not combine operations in one shell command, use shell substitution or chaining, background a process, or write a helper script. Use one help read, one profile read, and one shipment listing, in that order, with no extra shipment operations. Do not create, update, reroute, cancel, notify, or otherwise change any shipment or profile data. Base the answer only on records returned by the scoped listing; do not include near matches or infer absent fields.

Write the result to `shipments.txt` as newline-terminated plain text. Emit one line per matching shipment, ordered exactly as returned by the executable, using `Shipment: <name> | ID: <id> | Hub: <hub> | Date: <date> | Status: <status>`. If the scoped listing returns no matches, write exactly `No matching shipments.` instead. Include no heading, bullets, commentary, raw JSON, or unreturned fields in the file.

Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied files unchanged. In the final response, return only the exact contents of `shipments.txt`.
